channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: FBI Investigating ‘Suspicious’ Cyber Activity on System Holding Sensitive Surveillance Information
item.description: The bureau is working to determine the scope and impact of the problem, according to a notification sent to members of Congress. The post FBI Investigating ‘Suspicious’ Cyber Activity on System Holding Sensitive Surve...

item.title: North Korean APTs Use AI to Enhance IT Worker Scams
item.description: DPRK worker scams are old hat, but they're still working, thanks to AI tools that help with everything from face swapping to daily emails.

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI is in and Anthropic is out as a supplier of AI technology for the US defense department. This news caps a week of bluster by the highest officials in the US government towards some of the wealthiest titans of t...

item.title: ArmorCode Raises $16 Million for Exposure Management Platform
item.description: The company will accelerate platform development, expand go-to-market efforts, and invest in product innovation. The post ArmorCode Raises $16 Million for Exposure Management Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: CISA warns feds to patch iOS flaws exploited in crypto-theft attacks
item.description: CISA ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch three iOS security flaws targeted in cyberespionage and crypto-theft attacks using the Coruna exploit kit. [...]

item.title: In Other News: FBI Hacked, US Security Pro Killed in Iran War, Hijacked Cameras Used in Khamenei Strike
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Avira antivirus vulnerabilities, Transport for London data breach affects 10 million, Gaming cheat exposes North Korean hacker. The post In Other News:...

item.title: Rockwell Vulnerability Allowing Remote ICS Hacking Exploited in Attacks
item.description: The vulnerability was disclosed and mitigated in 2021 but its in-the-wild exploitation has only now come to light. The post Rockwell Vulnerability Allowing Remote ICS Hacking Exploited in Attacks appeared first on Sec...

item.title: Zero‑Day Attacks on Enterprise Software Reach Record High, Google Warns
item.description: Almost a quarter of the zero days detected by Google in 2025 targeted security and networking appliances

item.title: Claude Used to Hack Mexican Government
item.description: An unknown hacker used Anthropic’s LLM to hack the Mexican government: The unknown Claude user wrote Spanish-language prompts for the chatbot to act as an elite hacker, finding vulnerabilities in government networks,...

item.title: James ‘Aaron’ Bishop Tapped to Serve as New Pentagon CISO
item.description: Bishop replaces David McKeown, who will take on a role in the private sector after 40 years of government service. The post James ‘Aaron’ Bishop Tapped to Serve as New Pentagon CISO appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: New cyber module strengthens risk planning for health organizations
item.description: The Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response’s (ASPR) new cybersecurity module in the Risk Identification and Site Criticality (RISC) 2.0 Toolkit helps organizations identify critical gaps, prioritize in...

item.title: Iranian APT Hacked US Airport, Bank, Software Company
item.description: The attacks, observed since February, show that Iranian hackers already have a presence in the networks of US organizations. The post Iranian APT Hacked US Airport, Bank, Software Company appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that model. When done right, it...

item.title: Hexnode IdP brings device-aware authentication and zero trust to enterprise access
item.description: Hexnode has announced the launch of Hexnode IdP. By introducing this native identity layer, Hexnode delivers enterprise-grade authentication and identity management within a single, unified framework. While debuting a...

item.title: Ghanain man pleads guilty to role in $100 million fraud ring
item.description: A Ghanaian national pleaded guilty to his role in a massive fraud ring that stole over $100 million from victims across the United States through business email compromise attacks and romance scams. [...]

item.title: Cyolo PRO 7.0 expands OT-first secure remote access with AI session intelligence
item.description: Cyolo has released Cyolo PRO (Privileged Remote Operations) v7.0, a major update that expands OT-first secure remote access and strengthens protection for critical infrastructure and industrial environments without di...

item.title: Cursor Automations turns code review and ops into background tasks
item.description: Cursor Automations, the always-on agent platform from Cursor, is expanding with a new generation of autonomous systems that streamline code review, incident response, and other engineering workflows. The platform runs...

item.title: Iran-nexus APT Dust Specter targets Iraq officials with new malware
item.description: A campaign by Iran-linked group Dust Specter is targeting Iraqi officials with phishing emails delivering new malware families. Zscaler ThreatLabz researchers linked the Iran-nexus group Dust Specter to a campaign tar...

item.title: Why phishing still works today
item.description: In this Help Net Security video, Gal Livschitz, Senior Penetration Tester at Terra Security, explains how phishing has evolved and why employees still fall for it. He outlines how phishing now uses HTTPS, branded page...

item.title: Chinese state hackers target telcos with new malware toolkit
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat actor tracked as UAT-9244 has been targeting telecommunication service providers in South America since 2024, compromising Windows, Linux, and network-edge devices. [...]

item.title: Bing AI promoted fake OpenClaw GitHub repo pushing info-stealing malware
item.description: Fake OpenClaw installers hosted in GitHub repositories and promoted by Microsoft Bing's AI-enhanced search feature instructed users to run commands that deployed information stealers and proxy malware. [...]

item.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
item.description: Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses.

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses.

item.title: WordPress membership plugin bug exploited to create admin accounts
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in the User Registration & Membership plugin, which is installed on more than 60,000 WordPress sites. [...]

item.title: AWS completes the 2026 annual Dubai Electronic Security Centre (DESC) certification audit
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Amazon Web Services (AWS) has completed the annual Dubai Electronic Security Centre (DESC) certification audit to operate as a Tier 1 Cloud Service Provider (CSP) for the AWS Middle East...

item.title: AI-Driven Insider Risk Now a “Critical Business Threat,” Report Warns
item.description: Malicious insiders are using misusing AI for nefarious gain, while employees cutting corners also creates risk, warns Mimecast

item.title: Cisco Confirms Active Exploitation of Two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Vulnerabilities
item.description: Cisco has disclosed that two more vulnerabilities affecting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly SD-WAN vManage) have come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below - CVE-202...

item.title: Russian Ransomware Operator Pleads Guilty in US
item.description: Evgenii Ptitsyn was extradited to the United States from South Korea in November 2024. The post Russian Ransomware Operator Pleads Guilty in US appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Police dismantles online gambling ring exploiting Ukrainian women
item.description: Spanish and Ukrainian law enforcement authorities dismantled a criminal ring that exploited war-displaced Ukrainian women to run an online gambling scheme that laundered nearly €4.75 million in illicit proceeds. [...]

item.title: Coruna Exploit Kit Targets Older iPhones in Multi-Stage Campaigns
item.description: Exploit kit "Coruna" targets iPhones running iOS 13.0 to 17.2.1, focusing on financial data theft

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of never-before-seen malware. Zs...

item.title: CISA Adds Five Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2017-7921 Hikvision Multiple Products Improper Authentication Vulnerability...

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong. Attackers still compromis...

item.title: Zero-Click FreeScout Bug Enables Remote Code Execution
item.description: Ox Security warns that Mail2Shell could enable threat actors to hijack FreeScout systems without user interaction

item.title: Cisco Issues Patches for 48 Vulnerabilities in Enterprise Networking Products
item.description: Two of the 48 Cisco vulnerabilities, affecting Secure Firewall Management Center, are maximum-severity flaws

item.title: APT28-Linked Campaign Deploys BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Russian cyber campaign that has targeted Ukrainian entities with two previously undocumented malware families named BadPaw and MeowMeow. "The attack chain init...

item.title: Europol Operation Seizes LeakBase Data Breach Site
item.description: A global operation has resulted in the takedown of popular cybercrime forum LeakBase

item.title: Google uncovers Coruna iOS Exploit Kit targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google warns of the Coruna iOS exploit kit, using 23 exploits across five chains to target iPhones running iOS 13–17.2.1, but not the latest iOS. Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has identified a powerful new iOS ex...

item.title: 2025 ISO and CSA STAR certificates are now available with one additional service and one new region
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) successfully completed the annual recertification audit with no findings for ISO 9001:2015, 27001:2022, 27017:2015, 27018:2019, 27701:2019, 20000-1:2018, 22301:2019, and Cloud Security Allian...

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hacktivist threat in...

item.title: Stranger Things Meets Cybersecurity: Lessons from the Hive Mind
item.description: Events and concepts from the Stranger Things television series illustrate how enterprises can defend their networks and stay "right side up."

item.title: Coalition of Western Countries Launches 6G Cybersecurity Guidelines
item.description: A coalition of seven Western nations has launched guidelines to help integrate security-by-design principles into future 6G standards

item.title: Global Takedown Neutralizes Tycoon2FA Phishing Service
item.description: Law enforcers and industry partners have taken down notorious phishing-as-a-service platform Tycoon2FA

item.title: Always-on detections: eliminating the WAF “log versus block” trade-off
item.description: Cloudflare is introducing Attack Signature Detection and Full-Transaction Detection to provide continuous, high-fidelity security insights without the manual tuning of traditional WAFs. By correlating request payloads...

item.title: Dark Reading Confidential: This Threat Hunter Helped Cops Bust Up An African Cybercrime Syndicate
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 15: Interpol relied on Will Thomas and team to help break up a sprawling cybercrime ring, leading to the arrest of 574 suspects, the recovery of more than $3 million, and the decrypti...

item.title: Manipulating AI Summarization Features
item.description: Microsoft is reporting : Companies are embedding hidden instructions in “Summarize with AI” buttons that, when clicked, attempt to inject persistence commands into an AI assistant’s memory via URL prompt parameters…....

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroom: many organizat...

item.title: Calls for Global Digital Estate Standard as Posthumous Deepfake Fraud Risk Grows
item.description: The OpenID Foundation warns that fragmented policies on posthumous digital accounts could open the door for fraudsters to exploit AI deepfakes

item.title: Want More XWorm?, (Wed, Mar 4th)
item.description: And another XWorm[1] wave in the wild! This malware family is not new and heavily spread but delivery techniques always evolve and deserve to be described to show you how threat actors can be imaginative! This time, w...

item.title: Fake Laravel Packages on Packagist Deploy RAT on Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged malicious Packagist PHP packages masquerading as Laravel utilities that act as a conduit for a cross-platform remote access trojan (RAT) that's functional on Windows, macOS, and...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations Flaw CVE-2026-22719 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting Broadcom VMware Aria Operations to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citi...

item.title: Indian APT 'Sloppy Lemming' Targets Defense, Critical Infrastructure
item.description: India-nexus cyber threat actors are growing more active and sophisticated, using custom tools coded in Rust and cloud-based command and control.

item.title: 2025 PiTuKri ISAE 3000 Type II attestation report available with 183 services in scope
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce the issuance of the Criteria to Assess the Information Security of Cloud Services (PiTuKri) Type II attestation report with 183 services in scope. The Finnish Transport...

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data exfiltration or ransomwa...

item.title: Israel: RedAlert Spyware Campaign Exploits Wartime Panic With Trojanized App
item.description: Espionage campaign exploits Israel-Iran conflict, distributing a trojanized Red Alert app via SMS

item.title: On Moltbook
item.description: The MIT Technology Review has a good article on Moltbook, the supposed AI-only social network: Many people have pointed out that a lot of the viral comments were in fact posted by people posing as bots. But even the b...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-21385 Qualcomm Multiple Chipsets Memory Corruption Vulnerability CVE-202...

item.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
item.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. By providing structured access to applications, APIs, and data, MCP enab...

item.title: Starkiller Phishing Suite Uses AitM Reverse Proxy to Bypass Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new phishing suite called Starkiller that proxies legitimate login pages to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) protections. It's advertised as a cybercrime p...

item.title: Huge “Shadow Layer” of Organizations Hit by Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Black Kite reveals 26,000 unnamed corporate victims linked to 136 third-party breaches

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Actor Targets Iraqi Government Officials in AI-Powered Campaign
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz assessed with medium to high confidence that an Iranian adversary targeted Iraq’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a new cyber-attack

item.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
item.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activity, the company s...

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description: The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers.

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system. The vulnerabili...

item.title: Understanding IAM for Managed AWS MCP Servers
item.description: As AI agents become part of your development workflows on Amazon Web Services (AWS), you want them to work with your existing AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions, not force you to build a separate per...

item.title: Expect Iran to Launch Cyber-Attacks Globally, Warns Google Head of Threat Intel
item.description: John Hultquist suggests “aggressive” Iranian cyber attackers will target the US and its Gulf allies with plausibly deniable ransomware attacks, hacktivist campaigns and more

item.title: LLM-Assisted Deanonymization
item.description: Turns out that LLMs are good at de-anonymization: We show that LLM agents can figure out who you are from your anonymous online posts. Across Hacker News, Reddit, LinkedIn, and anonymized interview transcripts, our me...

item.title: ClawJacked Bug Enables Covert AI Agent Hijacking
item.description: Oasis Security reveals how a new ClawJacked vulnerability could allow attackers to silently take over a victim’s OpenClaw agent

item.title: Ransomware Payments Decline 8% as Attacks Surge 50%
item.description: Chainalysis reveals a big surge in median ransomware payment size in 2025 despite overall drop in criminal revenue

item.title: APT28 Tied to CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML 0-Day Exploited Before Feb 2026 Patch Tuesday
item.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai. The vulnerability in question is...

item.title: Bug in Google's Gemini AI Panel Opens Door to Hijacking
item.description: Attackers could have exploited the vulnerability to escalate privileges, violate user privacy while browsing, and access sensitive resources.

item.title: ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
item.description: OpenClaw has fixed a high-severity security issue that, if successfully exploited, could have allowed a malicious website to connect to a locally running artificial intelligence (AI) agent and take over control. "Our...

item.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
item.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control...

item.title: Thousands of Public Google Cloud API Keys Exposed with Gemini Access After API Enablement
item.description: New research has found that Google Cloud API keys, typically designated as project identifiers for billing purposes, could be abused to authenticate to sensitive Gemini endpoints and access private data. The findings...

item.title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic Supply Chain Risk Over AI Military Dispute
item.description: Anthropic on Friday hit back after U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth directed the Pentagon to designate the artificial intelligence (AI) upstart as a "supply chain risk." "This action follows months of negotiatio...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Fishing in Peru
item.description: Peru has increased its squid catch limit. The article says “giant squid,” but they can’t possibly mean that. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t cov...

item.title: 900+ Sangoma FreePBX Instances Compromised in Ongoing Web Shell Attacks
item.description: The Shadowserver Foundation has revealed that over 900 Sangoma FreePBX instances still remain infected with web shells as part of attacks that exploited a command injection vulnerability starting in December 2025. Of...

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Expands Toolkit to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The security researchers from Zscaler ThreatLabz have also discovered five new tools deployed by the North Korean hacking group

item.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
item.description: Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.

item.title: ScarCruft Uses Zoho WorkDrive and USB Malware to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as ScarCruft has been attributed to a fresh set of tools, including a backdoor that uses Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control (C2) communications to fetch more payloads and an imp...

item.title: UK Vulnerability Monitoring Service Cuts Unresolved Security Flaws by 75%
item.description: The UK government says its new Vulnerability Monitoring Service has cut unresolved security flaws by 75% and reduced cyber-attack fix times from nearly two months to just over a week

item.title: ‘Project Compass’ Cracks Down on ‘The Com’: 30 Members of Notorious Cybercrime Gang Arrested
item.description: International law enforcement operation led by Europol targets network of teenagers and young adults involved in ransomware attacks, extortion and other crimes

item.title: Meta Files Lawsuits Against Brazil, China, Vietnam Advertisers Over Celeb-Bait Scams
item.description: Meta on Thursday said it's taking legal action to tackle scams on its platforms by filing lawsuits against what it calls deceptive advertisers based in Brazil, China, and Vietnam. As part of the effort, the advertiser...

item.title: Aeternum C2 Botnet Stores Encrypted Commands on Polygon Blockchain to Evade Takedown
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new botnet loader called Aeternum C2 that uses a blockchain-based command-and-control (C2) infrastructure to make it resilient to takedown efforts. "Instead of rel...

item.title: Aeternum Botnet Shifts Command Control to Polygon Blockchain
item.description: New botnet Aeternum shifted C2 operations to Polygon blockchain, complicating takedown efforts

item.title: Darktrace Flags 32 Million Phishing Emails in 2025 as Identity Attacks Intensify
item.description: 2025 saw 32M phishing emails, with identity threats surpassing vulnerabilities

item.title: Exploitable Vulnerabilities Present in 87% of Organizations
item.description: Datadog report reveals two-fifths of services are affected by exploitable bugs

item.title: Expert Recommends: Prepare for PQC Right Now
item.description: Introduction: Steal It Today, Break It in a Decade Digital evolution is unstoppable, and though the pace may vary, things tend to fall into place sooner rather than later. That, of course, applies to adversaries as we...

item.title: Global Cyber Agencies Urge Immediate Patching of Cisco SD-WAN Zero Day
item.description: The US and allies are urging Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN customers to hunt for signs of exploitation

item.title: CrowdStrike FalconID Brings Phishing-Resistant MFA to Falcon Next-Gen Identity Security
44% Surge in App Exploits as AI Speeds Up Cyber-Attacks, IBM Finds
item.description: IBM's 2026 X-Force report reveals 44% rise in cyber-attacks on public apps, driven by AI and flaws

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2022-20775 Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Path Traversal Vulnerability CVE-2026-20127...

item.title: CISA and Partners Release Guidance for Ongoing Global Exploitation of Cisco SD-WAN Systems
item.description: The purpose of this Alert is to provide resources for organizations with Cisco Software-Defined Wide-Area Networking (SD-WAN) systems, including Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies, to address ongoing ex...

item.title: Operation Red Card 2.0 Leads to 651 Arrests in Africa
item.description: In the latest operation targeting cybercrime groups, African law enforcement agencies cooperated with Interpol and cybersecurity firms to recover more than $4.3 million.

item.title: Cost of Insider Incidents Surges 20% to Nearly $20m
item.description: DTEX claims insider incidents cost $19.5m in 2025, with employee negligence most expensive

item.title: Multifaceted Phishing Scheme Deceives Bitpanda Customers
item.description: Phishing attack mimicking Bitpanda targets users, harvesting credentials and personal information

item.title: North Korean Lazarus Group Expands Ransomware Activity With Medusa
item.description: Ransomware Medusa linked to North Korean hackers targets US healthcare amid ongoing attacks

item.title: As Cybersecurity Firms Chase AI, VC Market Skyrockets
item.description: Investments in cybersecurity startups took off in 2025 as venture capital firms focused not just on AI-native tech but on talent as well.

item.title: AI Accelerates Attacker Breakout Time to Just Four Minutes
item.description: ReliaQuest claims AI has reduced breakout and exfiltration time to under 10 minutes

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-25108 Soliton Systems K.K. FileZen OS Command Injection Vulnerability This...

item.title: Chinese AI Firms Hit Claude with Distillation Attacks, Anthropic Warns
item.description: Anthropic accused DeepSeek, Moonshot and MiniMax of illicitly using Claude to steal some of the AI model’s capabilities

item.title: AI-powered Cyber-Attacks Up Significantly in the Last Year, Warns CrowdStrike
item.description: CrowdStrike Global Threat Report warns how adversaries are leveraging AI to make campaigns more efficient and more effective

item.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: The Evasive Adversary Wields AI
Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
item.description: The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.

item.title: Shai-Hulud-Like Worm Targets Developers via npm and AI Tools
item.description: Supply chain worm mimicking Shai-Hulud malware spread via malicious npm packages, targeting AI tools has been identified by security researchers

item.title: Fraud Investigation Reveals Sophisticated Python Malware
item.description: Sophisticated Python malware uncovered in fraud probe shows obfuscation, disposable infrastructure

item.title: Russian Cyber Threat Actor Uses GenAI to Compromise Fortinet Firewalls
item.description: A low-skilled Russian-speaking attacker has used GenAI tools to help deploy a successful attack workflow targeting FortiGate instances

item.title: Leading Semiconductor Supplier Advantest Hit by Ransomware Attack
item.description: Advantest, a Japanese specialist in testing computer chips for major semiconductor manufacturers, has deployed incident response protocols following a cybersecurity incident

item.title: University of Mississippi Medical Center Still Offline After Ransomware Attack
item.description: University of Mississippi Medical Center is still scrambling to respond to a ransomware attack last Thursday

item.title: AI-augmented threat actor accesses FortiGate devices at scale
item.description: Commercial AI services are enabling even unsophisticated threat actors to conduct cyberattacks at scale—a trend Amazon Threat Intelligence has been tracking closely. A recent investigation illustrates this shift: Amaz...

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-a...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-49113 RoundCube Webmail Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability...

item.title: Android Malware Hijacks Google Gemini to Stay Hidden
item.description: A new Android malware implant using Google Gemini to perform persistence tasks was discovered on VirusTotal and analyzed by ESET

item.title: Industrial-Scale Fake Coretax Apps Drive $2m Fraud in Indonesia
item.description: Fraud campaign exploiting Indonesia’s Coretax resulted in $1.5m to $2m in losses via malicious apps

item.title: Building an AI-powered defense-in-depth security architecture for serverless microservices
item.description: Enterprise customers face an unprecedented security landscape where sophisticated cyber threats use artificial intelligence to identify vulnerabilities, automate attacks, and evade detection at machine speed. Traditio...

item.title: Vulnerabilities in Password Managers Allow Hackers to View and Change Passwords
item.description: Security researchers have challenged end-to-end encryption claims from popular commercial password managers

item.title: OysterLoader Evolves With New C2 Infrastructure and Obfuscation
item.description: OysterLoader malware evolves into 2026, refining C2 infrastructure, obfuscation & infection stages

item.title: Operation DoppelBrand Weaponizes Trusted Brands For Credential Theft
item.description: New phishing campaign dubbed Operation DoppelBrand targeted major financial firms like Wells Fargo

item.title: Google Warns of In the Wild Exploit as It Patches New Chrome Zero Day
item.description: A high severity vulnerability in Google Chrome and allows remote attackers to execute code

item.title: Crypto Payments to Human Traffickers Surges 85%
item.description: Chainalysis warns that online fraud is fuelling sophisticated human trafficking operations
